meta.title: Argentine Media Ownership Map — Wave 1
meta.description: Cross-ownership of media, energy, telecom, and finance sectors; political connections; offshore entities; pauta oficial flows.

groups.name: Grupo Clarín S.A.

shareholders.name: Héctor Magnetto
shareholders.notes: Controls via trust in New York

shareholders.name: Noble Herrera family
shareholders.notes: Adopted children of Ernestina Herrera de Noble; trust in New York

groups.holding_structure: Two trusts in New York (Noble family, Magnetto) + GS Unidos LLC (Delaware) for Herrera de Noble family. Bicephalous: Grupo Clarín S.A. (media/content) and Cablevisión Holding (telecom/networks via Telecom Argentina).

paper: Papel Prensa S.A. (shared with La Nación and state)

pending_acquisition.status: Suspended by Milei government; ENACOM/CNDC review pending as of March 2026
pending_acquisition.monopoly_risk: Would give ~61% mobile market, ~69% fixed telephony

political_connections: Magnetto negotiated judicial appointments with Macri government (Lake Escondido scandal)
War with Kirchner governments over Ley de Medios (2009-2015)
Telefónica acquisition suspended by Milei government (2025) — rare friction with libertarian administration
Controlled newspaper paper supply (Papel Prensa) giving leverage over competitors
Network of links with judges and prosecutors documented

pauta_oficial.2023: Over USD 11 million in official advertising (largest recipient)
pauta_oficial.2024_national: 10.7% of total national pauta (largest conglomerate share), but national spending cut to 1.2% of 2023 levels under Milei
pauta_oficial.2024_caba: 10.7% of Buenos Aires City advertising budget (Jorge Macri spent ~$100 billion ARS total)
pauta_oficial.notes: YPF 'paraestatal' advertising (~$100B ARS in 2024) became main channel after Milei cut direct pauta

groups.name: Grupo América

shareholders.name: José Luis Manzano
shareholders.notes: Ex-Minister of Interior under Menem (1991-92); Integra Capital founder

shareholders.name: Gustavo Scaglione
shareholders.notes: Entered Nov 2024, acquired Gabriel Hochbaum's stake

newspapers: Diario UNO (Mendoza, Santa Fe, Entre Ríos)
media_assets.notes: 40 companies in 17 provinces; second-largest media group

energy: Edenor (largest electricity distributor in South America, acquired 2021 for ~USD 200M)
Andes Energía / Phoenix Global Resources (oil, 24 licenses, 13 with YPF, 6 in Vaca Muerta)
mining: 243,000 hectares of lithium salt flats in Catamarca and Jujuy
Volcan Cia Minera SAA (Peru, acquired from Glencore)
telecom_international: Telefónica del Perú (99.3% acquired April 2025, with Mauricio Filiberti)
healthcare: Swiss Medical Group (Belocopitt — one of Argentina's largest private health providers)
media_international: El Universo (Ecuador, acquired Feb 2026 by Manzano)

political_connections: Manzano: Minister of Interior under Menem (1991-92), known for phrase 'robo para la corona'
Close relationship with Sergio Massa (invited to private events)
Edenor purchase approved by Fernández government (ENRE Resolution 207/2021)
Vila-Manzano attempted to acquire Grupo Indalo media during Macri persecution of López/De Sousa
Bloomberg Línea named both Vila and Manzano among most influential people in Latin America
Manzano described as 'adapting to every government' — menemista, then Kirchnerista relationships, now Milei-era expansion

groups.name: Grupo Indalo

shareholders.name: Cristóbal López
shareholders.notes: Patagonian businessman; acquitted in Oil Combustibles case (Oct 2023)

shareholders.name: Fabián De Sousa
shareholders.notes: Former partner; López took control of Indalo Medios, De Sousa separated

production: Ideas del Sur (Marcelo Tinelli's production company, acquired 2013)
media_assets.notes: All purchased from Daniel Hadad in 2012 for USD 49.5M (C5N + Radio 10 + 4 FMs). Currently managed by official receivers.

fuel: Oil Combustibles (340 gas stations; went bankrupt, bankruptcy finalized Aug 2021 after 98% creditor approval)
non_media_assets.notes: At peak, 170 companies across fuel, construction, media, food, casinos, hotels, banking

political_connections: Considered 'empresario K' — closely aligned with Kirchner governments
Oil Combustibles benefited from deferred tax payments on fuel transfer tax (ITC) during Kirchner era
AFIP head Ricardo Echegaray allowed repeated refinancing of tax debt
Macri government prosecuted López and De Sousa, both imprisoned (later acquitted Oct 2023)
Vila-Manzano attempted hostile takeover of Indalo media during Macri-era persecution
Magnetto/Macri alliance allegedly sought to acquire C5N

tax_debt.oil_combustibles: $6.7 billion ARS (fuel transfer tax ITC withheld but not remitted)
tax_debt.outcome: Acquitted — court ruled no crime committed by Oil Combustibles or shareholders

groups.current_status: López and De Sousa split partnership. Grupo Indalo preparing relaunch. Companies still partially under official receivers.

groups.name: S.A. La Nación

shareholders.name: Saguier family
shareholders.notes: Via MNMS Holding S.A. (Matilde Noble Mitre de Saguier Holding)

groups.holding_structure: MNMS Holding S.A. controls 62.95% of shares. Saguiers purchased majority from Mitre family in the 1990s. Matilde Noble Mitre de Saguier (Julio/Alejandro's mother) is niece of Roberto Noble, founder of Clarín — cross-family link.

paper: Papel Prensa S.A. (shared with Clarín and state)
media_assets.notes: La Nación journalists (Hugo Alconada Mon) were part of ICIJ Pandora Papers team but delayed reporting on Saguier offshore

offshore.purpose: Controls family share within MNMS Holding

political_connections: Historically conservative/liberal editorial line, aligned with agricultural/business elite
Saguier family connection to Noble family (Clarín founder)
Alejandro Saguier declared offshore via Macri's 2016 tax amnesty (blanqueo)
Saguier and Escribano (La Nación directors) received partial state salaries

groups.name: Grupo Octubre S.A.

shareholders.name: Víctor Santa María
shareholders.notes: Secretary General of SUTERH (building workers union)

shareholders.name: SUTERH
shareholders.notes: Sindicato Único de Trabajadores de Edificios de Renta y Horizontal — union owns group via Fundación Octubre de Trabajadores

education: UMET (Universidad Metropolitana para la Educación y el Trabajo)

political_connections: Santa María is a Peronist union leader — SUTERH represents building doormen/maintenance workers
Close alignment with Kirchnerism and Peronist governments
Página/12 editorial line strongly pro-Kirchner
Union funds used to build media empire — raises conflict of interest questions
Grupo Octubre among top pauta oficial recipients under Alberto Fernández government

financial_investigations.personal_wealth_abroad: Over USD 12 million (Santa María family); USD 6 million (Santa María personally) per UIF investigation
financial_investigations.suspicious_operations: Investigated for millions in transfers to union supplier Andrés Stangalini
financial_investigations.labor_issues_2025: Non-payment of wages at Página/12 for months; 50-60 layoffs at IP; salary arrears at Canal 9

groups.name: Editorial Perfil
political_connections: Fontevecchia positions Perfil as 'independent' / centrist
Has published investigative pieces critical of both Kirchnerism and Macrismo
Declared offshore entities after Pandora Papers revelation

groups.name: Grupo Infobae

shareholders.name: Viviana Zocco
shareholders.notes: Wife; partner in Grupo Vi-Da (TKM, BajaLibros)

media_assets.former_assets: Sold C5N + Radio 10 + 4 FM stations to Cristóbal López in 2012 for USD 49.5M

offshore.notes: Linked to 8 companies in tax havens per investigative reports

political_connections: Hadad claims Kirchner government pressured him to sell C5N/Radio 10 via AFIP raids (20 inspectors sent to his accountant's office)
Post-sale pivot to digital-only model with Infobae — now dominant digital platform
Editorial line generally center-right, critical of Kirchnerism
López disputes Hadad's pressure narrative, claims Hadad pressured him to buy

pandora_papers_media_owners: Héctor Magnetto (Clarín) — trust in New York
Alejandro Saguier (La Nación) — Danias Holdings Ltd, BVI (2012); declared via 2016 blanqueo
Saguier family — Matilde de Saguier Corp, BVI (1998)
Jorge Fontevecchia (Perfil) — Akriluy Corporation SA, Uruguay (2008); Perfil Exterior, BVI; Perfil Asia, Hong Kong

media_energy_nexus.description: Vila-Manzano is the most dramatic example: media (Grupo América/Telefe) + energy (Edenor, Metrogas, Andes Energía, Vaca Muerta) + mining (lithium, potash) + international telecom (Telefónica del Perú). Clarín's telecom dominance (Telecom Argentina/Personal/Fibertel + pending Movistar acquisition) represents the media-telecom nexus.

pauta_oficial_summary.kirchner_era: Heavy state advertising to aligned media (Indalo, Octubre leading recipients alongside Clarín)
pauta_oficial_summary.milei_era: Official 'pauta cero' policy — but YPF spent ~$100B ARS in 2024 as 'paraestatal' channel. Clarín still received 10.7% of remaining national pauta. Jorge Macri (CABA) spent ~$100B ARS in city advertising.
pauta_oficial_summary.key_insight: Pauta oficial never truly disappears — it migrates to state enterprises (YPF) or subnational governments when national government cuts it

transactional_all_governments: Grupo América (Vila-Manzano) — adapts to whoever is in power

hadad_lopez_connection.description: In 2012, Hadad sold C5N + Radio 10 + 4 FMs to Cristóbal López for USD 49.5M. Hadad claims AFIP pressure under Kirchner government (20 inspectors raided his accountant). López claims Hadad pressured him. The sale transformed C5N from center-right to pro-Kirchner overnight. Hadad pivoted to digital-only Infobae.
hadad_lopez_connection.afip_role: AFIP head Ricardo Echegaray allegedly facilitated both the pressure on Hadad and the deferred tax payments for López's Oil Combustibles
